name: nishang
summary: Nishang is a framework and collection of scripts and payloads which enables usage of PowerShell for offensive security, penetration testing and red teaming. Nishang is useful during all phases of penetration testing. By Nikhil Mittal Founder of Altered Security - Hands-on red team and enterprise security training! Usage

documentation.readme: Nishang is a framework and collection of scripts and payloads which enables usage of PowerShell for offensive security, penetration testing and red teaming. Nishang is useful during all phases of penetration testing. By Nikhil Mittal Founder of Altered Security - Hands-on red team and enterprise security training! Usage

arguments.name: --depth
arguments.description: CLI flag '--depth' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

arguments.name: --filter
arguments.description: BPF filter expression for packet selection.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Offensive PowerShell framework category. Fits when the engagement needs: Nishang is a framework and collection of scripts and payloads which enables usage of PowerShell for offensive security, penetration testing and red teaming. Nishang is useful during all phases of penetration testing. By .
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Nishang is a framework and collection of scripts and payloads which enables usage of PowerShell for offensive security, penetration testing and red teaming. Nishang is useful during all phases of penetration testing. By Nikhil Mittal Founder of Altered Security - Hands-on red team and enterprise security training! Usage.
documentation.how_to_use:
Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `PS C:
ishang> Import-Module .
ishang.psm1`. Further invocations from upstream: `PS C:
ishang> . C:
ishang\Gather\Get-Information.ps1`; `PS C:
ishang> Get-Information`. Nishang is a framework and collection of scripts and payloads which enables usage of PowerShell for offensive security, penetration testing and red teaming. Nishang is useful during all phases of penetration testing. By Nikhil Mittal Founder of Altered Security - Hands-on red team and enterprise security training! Usag.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
